source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Lead
source_jobs.description:
We’re building a world-class team to redefine knowledge work with AI

Zeno is a legal AI startup building a platform that helps lawyers research, review, and draft documents with real legal reasoning — not just text prediction. We’re developing technology that can:

 - Search and retrieve statutes, case law, and commentary with high precision.

 - Reason step-by-step, applying legal tests and weighing precedents.

 - Explain every answer transparently, so lawyers can trace conclusions back to the exact sources.

Where most tools automate surface-level tasks, we’re focused on replicating the way lawyers actually think through legal problems, making depth and trust the foundation of everything we build.

You’re joining an early-stage startup that is already working with leading firms. Backed with €3M in seed funding, we’re now scaling a team of engineers and thinkers who want to solve real problems, drive innovation, and create lasting change in the legal sector.



The role

Own the strategy that connects our product to the market.

Zeno builds agentic AI software for legal professionals. We're scaling across Benelux and into new markets and we need a Product Marketing Lead who can make sure every team is working from the same, accurate picture of who we serve, why we win and what we should build next.

This role sits at the boundary between product and go-to-market. On the product side, you feed customer insights, market signals and adoption data into roadmap decisions. On the GTM side, you ensure that positioning, messaging, pricing and enablement are coherent across Sales, Marketing, CS and RevOps. You are not a support function for other teams: you set the strategic direction they all work from.

This is a senior individual contributor role with broad ownership and high autonomy. You will work with AI tools, freelancers and agencies to extend your output without the overhead of managing a large team.



What You'll Own

 - ICP definition and segmentation: who we serve, what drives them to buy and what signals matter most

 - Positioning and messaging across website narrative, sales materials and product touchpoints

 - Pricing and packaging strategy, including sensitivity analysis and ongoing iteration with Sales and CS

 - Product and feature launches: planning, cross-team alignment, assets and post-launch adoption tracking

 - Sales enablement: pitch narratives, battlecards, objection handling and competitive positioning

 - Customer and market research programs: structured interviews, win/loss analysis and competitive reviews

 - Product adoption strategy across the full customer lifecycle, from activation through expansion

 - Market entry and regional expansion planning



What You'll Do Day-to-Day

 - Run regular customer and prospect interviews; synthesize findings into positioning and product input

 - Write, test and iterate core messaging: value propositions, website copy and sales narratives

 - Run structured win/loss reviews and maintain a live competitive analysis

 - Build and manage launch plans: define the narrative, produce the assets and align the teams

 - Work with Product to define activation milestones and identify adoption friction across the customer journey

 - Translate product usage data and customer feedback into clear input for the roadmap

 - Use AI to accelerate research synthesis, generate and test messaging variants and automate documentation tasks



What You Bring

 - 5+ years in product marketing in B2B SaaS, with a track record of owning positioning and launches

 - Experience with ICP and customer research frameworks (e.g. SPICED, Jobs-to-be-Done, or similar)

 - Strong written communication: you make complex ideas simple and compelling without losing accuracy

 - Commercial instinct: you understand what moves win rates, adoption and expansion metrics

 - Experience working closely with Product, Sales, CS and RevOps teams

 - Active user of AI tools for research, drafting and analysis,

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager Legal & Partnerships, Germany
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ZENO

Zeno is a legal AI startup building a platform that helps lawyers, notaries and other legal

professionals research, review, and draft documents with real legal reasoning, not

just text prediction. We’re developing technology that can:



 - Search and retrieve statutes, case law, and commentary with high precision.

 - Reason step-by-step, applying legal tests and weighing precedents.

 - Explain every answer transparently, so legal professionals can trace conclusions back to the exact sources.



Where most tools automate surface-level tasks, we’re focused on replicating the way

lawyers actually think through legal problems, making depth and trust the foundation of

everything we build.



You’re joining an early-stage startup that is already working with leading firms. Backed with

€3M in seed funding, we’re now scaling a team that wants to solve real problems, drive

innovation, and create lasting change in the legal sector.




THE ROLE


HELP BUILDING A WORLD-CLASS TEAM TO REDEFINE KNOWLEDGE WORK WITH AI

We are looking for a German-qualified lawyer to lead Zeno's legal partnerships and legal affairs in Germany.

You will be part of the global Legal & Partnerships team and work closely with the founders, product team, and Head of Legal & Partnerships to support Zeno's growth in the German market.

This is a unique role that combines external market development with substantive legal work. You will help build relationships with leading law firms, legal departments and notaries, while also serving as a trusted legal partner within Zeno on commercial, regulatory, compliance, and strategic matters.

As Zeno's first senior legal hire focused on Germany, you will help shape our market strategy, legal positioning, partnership approach and internal legal function. You will work at the intersection of law, technology, product, and business, helping ensure that our growth is commercially successful and legally sound.

This role is ideal for someone who has practiced law, understands the German legal market, and wants to apply that expertise in a fast-growing legal technology company.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

You will take ownership of Zeno’s early legal partnerships in Germany.

In practice, that means you will:

 - build and manage relationships with law firms, notaries, and legal departments in Germany;

 - support legal, regulatory, and compliance matters relevant to operating an AI company in Germany and Europe;

 - help develop internal policies, legal processes, and governance frameworks;

 - identify where Zeno can create real value in legal research, drafting, review, and knowledge work;

 - collaborate with product and engineering teams on legal questions relating to AI, data protection, and responsible product development;

 - work with product and legal teams to bring market feedback back into the company;

 - serve as a legal subject matter expert internally and help ensure that Zeno maintains the high standards expected by legal professionals.

 - represent Zeno at legal tech events, law firm meetings and relevant industry conversations

You will not be handed a rigid playbook. You will help write it.




WHO YOU ARE

You are a qualified lawyer, legal counsel or senior legal professional with strong exposure to sophisticated legal work and a deep understanding of the German legal market.

You enjoy practicing law, but you are equally excited by the opportunity to help shape how technology transforms the legal profession. You want to apply your legal expertise beyond traditional practice and work at the intersection of law, product, strategy and business.

You likely bring:

 - strong German legal market knowledge and an understanding of how law firms, legal departments, and legal professionals evaluate new technologies;

 - experience at a leading law firm, legal department, legal tech company, or legal innovation team;

 - excellent German and English communication skills;

 - strong legal judgment and the a
